summary: PR #2195 primarily advances decompilation of src/melee/it/items/itbombhei.c, adding several BombHei item logic and animation/collision functions, updating BombHei item vars and attributes, and making small related item/fighter/stage formatting or field-name changes. Bot output reports +6020 matched-code bytes, 11 new matches, and 7 improved unmatched BombHei functions; the author also notes the file is mostly done but several functions remained below 100%. Review discussion centered on whether a file-local GET_ATTRS(ip) macro for item attributes is worthwhile or consistent enough to keep.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation target; many BombHei item functions replaced from placeholder comments with C implementations, includes added, GET_ATTRS macro introduced, state-update call forms adjusted, ABS/M_PI/lb helper use added, and PAD_STACK appears in several functions.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/it/items/itbombhei.c +376/-21, 15 hunks

what_changed: Added C implementations for many previously placeholder BombHei functions in src/melee/it/items/itbombhei.c, including dropped/thrown logic, state transitions, animation callbacks, collision callbacks, and helper functions.

Bot report recorded 11 new matches in main/melee/it/items/itbombhei: it_8027F8E0, fn_8027FCA8, it_3F14_Logic6_Thrown, it_3F14_Logic6_Dropped, it_80280B60, it_8027E978, it_8027EE04, it_8027F42C, it_3F14_Logic6_EnteredAir, .sdata2, and it_8027D670.

Bot report recorded 7 improved but still unmatched BombHei functions: itBombhei_UnkMotion4_Anim, fn_8028007C, itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Anim, itBombhei_UnkMotion3_Anim, itBombhei_UnkMotion10_Anim, itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Coll, and fn_80280974.
Updated BombHei structs in itCommonItems.h: xDF4 and attribute xC became float fields, _E04 became f32 xE04, and itBombHeiAttributes gained Vec3 x20.
Replaced some references to it_804D6D28->x30 with it_804D6D28->x30_lifetime in item code.
Introduced a local GET_ATTRS(ip) macro in itbombhei.c for casting ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes to itBombHeiAttributes*.

Used helper/macros and APIs visible in the diff such as GET_ITEM, HSD_JObjAddRotationY, HSD_JObjAddTranslationY, HSD_JObjAddRotationX, HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll, ABS, M_PI, lb_800119DC, and item state update helpers.
Made small formatting or cleanup edits in ft_0C31.c/.h, grinishie2.c, and it_27CF.c, with additional small touched files listed but not fully visible in the loaded diff.

smart_moves.text: Kept the main decompilation target focused on itbombhei.c while updating shared BombHei field types needed by the new implementation.
evidence_refs: changed_files: itbombhei.c +376/-21; itCommonItems.h +4/-3
diff: itBombHei_ItemVars and itBombHeiAttributes fields updated and then used by itbombhei.c

smart_moves.text: Used a typed BombHei attribute cast rather than repeating the full specialAttributes cast at every access, improving readability where it was used.
evidence_refs: diff: #define GET_ATTRS(ip) ((itBombHeiAttributes*) ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes)
review_comment by jurrejelle: macro helped readability compared with repeating the full cast

smart_moves.text: Recorded progress with both bot report and easy_funcs output, distinguishing fully matched functions from functions that remained partial.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report: 11 new matches and 7 improvements
human_pr_text: easy_funcs table with remaining functions at 90.32% to 96.15%

decomp_lessons.lesson: For item-specific attributes, a local typed cast helper can improve readability, but if matching requires inconsistent exceptions, reviewers may prefer avoiding or further investigating the pattern instead of treating it as a standard.
evidence_refs: review_comment ribbanya: asked whether the pattern meaningfully fixes the stack and noted no macro standard was defined
review_comment jurrejelle: said GET_ATTRS mostly helped readability but had to be broken a few times for permuter reasons
review_comment ribbanya: if not consistent it should be avoided; needs further investigation

decomp_lessons.lesson: Partial decomp PRs can land meaningful progress when they clearly identify remaining unmatched functions and preserve exact match evidence for completed functions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report: +6020 matched-code bytes, 11 new matches, 7 improvements
human_pr_text: author says mostly done with a few functions struggled with and includes easy_funcs percentages below 100%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Recovering field types in shared item structs can enable clearer decompiled code, such as using float fields and Vec3 attributes instead of integer placeholders.
evidence_refs: diff: itBombHei_ItemVars xDF4 s32->float and _E04->f32 xE04
diff: itBombHeiAttributes xC s32->float and Vec3 x20 added
diff: itbombhei.c uses attrs->xC in velocity math and attrs->x20.x/y in ABS bounds checks

naming_conventions.name: itBombHeiAttributes / itBombHei_ItemVars
naming_conventions.convention: BombHei-specific structs retain item kind prefix and offset-style field names where semantic names are not yet proven.
evidence_refs: diff: typedef struct itBombHei_ItemVars with xDD4/xDE8/xDEC/xDF0/xDF4/xE04/xE0C fields
diff: typedef struct itBombHeiAttributes with x0/x4/x8/xC/x10/x14/x18/x1C/x20 fields

naming_conventions.name: x30_lifetime
naming_conventions.convention: Shared item data field x30 is renamed/referenced as x30_lifetime where used as an item lifetime value.
evidence_refs: diff: it_802742F4 changes it_804D6D28->x30 to x30_lifetime
diff: Item_80267AA8 changes it_804D6D28->x30 to x30_lifetime
diff: itbombhei.c assigns ip->xD44_lifeTimer from it_804D6D28->x30_lifetime

naming_conventions.name: GET_ATTRS(ip)
naming_conventions.convention: File-local macro name used for item-specific attributes in this PR, but review explicitly says this was not an established standard.
evidence_refs: diff: #define GET_ATTRS(ip) ((itBombHeiAttributes*) ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes)
review_comment ribbanya: Fighters do this as well but we didn't define macros for it
review_comment jurrejelle: copied it over from itlikelike and was unaware it was not a standard

review_comment ribbanya: if not consistent it should be avoided

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used existing project/source constructs such as ABS for threshold checks and HSD_JObj helper calls for joint rotation/translation operations.
evidence_refs: diff: ABS(ip->xDD4_itemVar.bombhei.xE0C.x) > attr->x20.x and ABS(...y) > attr->x20.y

assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Consistent with loaded standards favoring macros and helper forms where they express the operation.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Some functions call itBombhei_UpdateStatePreserveBoneFake while others call itBombhei_UpdateStatePreserveBone; the diff suggests helper selection affected matching.
evidence_refs: diff: itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Anim and itBombhei_UnkMotion4_Anim use itBombhei_UpdateStatePreserveBoneFake

assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The loaded PR slice does not explain the fake helper's intended semantics or validation beyond overall match reports.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned whether the GET_ATTRS macro pattern meaningfully fixes the stack, noted uncertainty about the pattern, and said fighters have similar access but no macro standard was defined. Mentioned a possible generic macro shape like GET_ATTRS(ip, itBombHeiAttributes) had been considered but not implemented.
evidence_refs: review_comments loaded file discussion_r2869356886

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Explained the macro was copied from itlikelike, helped readability, and sometimes had to be broken for permuter reasons to get 100%.
evidence_refs: review_comments loaded file discussion_r2869406510

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Said that if the macro pattern is not consistent it should be avoided, needs further investigation, and that in the meantime using it or not does not matter much.
evidence_refs: review_comments loaded file discussion_r2869424116

accepted_candidate_records.title: Do not treat file-local GET_ATTRS item macros as a standard without consistency evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2195, a BombHei-local GET_ATTRS(ip) macro improved readability for repeated item attribute casts, but review questioned whether it meaningfully fixed stack/codegen and noted no project macro standard existed. The author said the pattern sometimes had to be broken for permuter reasons; reviewer response was that inconsistent use should be avoided pending further investigation.
evidence_refs: review_comments discussion_r2869356886
review_comments discussion_r2869406510
review_comments discussion_r2869424116

accepted_candidate_records.title: BombHei PR combined new 100% matches with explicitly tracked partial functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2195's bot report records 11 new matches in main/melee/it/items/itbombhei and 7 improved but still unmatched functions. The author also posted easy_funcs output showing several functions between roughly 90% and 96%, making remaining work searchable rather than implying full completion.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report issuecomment-3979906257
human_pr_text issuecomment-3980344893

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify policy for item attribute accessor macros
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding guidance that item-specific attribute accessor macros such as GET_ATTRS(ip) are not automatically accepted as a global pattern. Require local consistency and matching evidence; if an accessor must be bypassed for permuter/codegen reasons, prefer investigating the underlying type/control-flow issue before promoting the macro style. A future generic form like GET_ATTRS(ip, Type) was mentioned as previously considered but not implemented.
evidence_refs: review_comments discussion_r2869356886: no macro standard defined; generic macro idea mentioned
review_comments discussion_r2869406510: author had to break pattern for permuter reasons
review_comments discussion_r2869424116: inconsistent pattern should be avoided and needs further investigation
source_update_candidates.reason: Review evidence exposes an unresolved style/matching question that may deserve standards guidance, but the PR does not establish a final accepted rule.

source_update_candidates.title: src/melee/it/items/itbombhei.c is BombHei item decomp target with mixed complete and partial matches in PR #2195
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2195 added many BombHei item logic/animation/collision implementations in src/melee/it/items/itbombhei.c. Bot report lists 11 new matches and 7 improved unmatched functions in main/melee/it/items/itbombhei.
evidence_refs: changed_files: itbombhei.c +376/-21
text_corpus bot report: +6020 matched-code bytes; 11 new matches; 7 improvements
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-level historical context; curator should decide whether current path facts already cover it and whether historical match status should be recorded.

source_update_candidates.title: Review BombHei struct field type updates
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2195 changed BombHei item vars and attributes: xDF4 from s32 to float, _E04 to f32 xE04, attribute xC from s32 to float, and added Vec3 x20. These fields are used in the added BombHei code for rotation/velocity/lifetime/bounds-related logic.
evidence_refs: diff: itCommonItems.h BombHei field changes
diff: itbombhei.c uses xE04, attrs->xC, attrs->x20.x, attrs->x20.y
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential data/type sheet update candidate, but the PR slice alone does not prove semantic field names beyond offsets and types.

rejection_notes: Do not promote GET_ATTRS(ip) as an accepted global item macro standard from this PR; review explicitly says no standard existed and inconsistent use needs further investigation.
Do not claim all BombHei functions matched; author easy_funcs output and bot report identify several functions still below 100%.
Do not infer exact changes for itfreeze.c, itgshell.c, itlikelike.c, itmatadogas.c, types.h, or itbombhei.static.h beyond changed-file metadata where loaded diff excerpts do not show the hunks.
Do not treat PAD_STACK usage as generally justified style without per-function objdiff/build evidence; the loaded slice shows usage but not detailed rationale.
